title: Build connected AI: Orchestrate tools and agents with registries and ADK
summary: Google presented the Agent Development Kit (ADK) and Agent Registry as tools for orchestrating agents that can connect to external data sources, tools, and other agents. The approach uses Model Context Protocol (MCP) servers to integrate with third-party SaaS and legacy systems, with support for both existing MCP servers and custom implementations via specification boost add-on.
writeup: This Google Cloud Tech talk presents the Agent Development Kit (ADK) alongside an Agent Registry for orchestrating multi-agent systems and integrating tools. It covers connecting agents to external data sources, using Model Context Protocol (MCP) servers, and converting existing APIs into agent-ready tools through specification boost. The focus is on the plumbing that links agents, registries, and tools so multi-agent systems can act on outside data.
